title: NEWS
text: GitHub Actions: refactor deploy workflow dev main branches (pr34) Schema simplification: replace separate raw.yaml + tidy.yaml per tool single unified schema.yaml. table entry now flat columns: list column carries raw name, tidy name, type, description, versions array. Config derives versioned raw tidy views unified source demand. Tool: refactor parse/tidy dispatch — subclasses can now override per-table parse_<tname>() tidy_<tname>() methods; falls back ftype-based dispatch otherwise. Add support txt-nohead csv-nohead-long file types. Tool1: add table4 (txt-nohead) table5 (csv-nohead-long) examples new file type support. Workflow1: add CLI workflow dispatcher (nemoverse_wf_dispatch) end--end CLI testing. inst/scripts/file_to_yaml.R: refactor generate schema.yaml skeletons sample files; output goes inst/config/tools/_tmp/ avoid overwriting real schemas.

title: Contribute
text: Adding new tool {nemo} child package consists following steps: Data Add sample files generated tool inst/extdata/<tool>/latest/. Usually top 10 rows (plus header) representative TSV file fine. DVC recommended tracking files version control. See vignette("devnotes") DVC notes. Schema Use inst/scripts/file_to_yaml.R generate skeleton schema.yaml sample files. script auto-detects raw column names types; fill : tidy — snake_case column name tidy output description — human-readable field description (tool documentation good source) versions — explicit array every tool version column appears (e.g. ['v1.2.3', 'latest']); use ['latest'] columns present current version Save result inst/config/tools/<tool>/schema.yaml. See vignette("structure") details schema format Config reads . Parsers Create R6 class R/<Tool>.R. Use Tool1 (R/Tool1.R) template — demonstrates full pattern initialize(), per-table parse_*() / tidy_*() methods, roxygen documentation @testexamples blocks.

title: Devnotes
text: Instead committing big data files git, can store files remotely (e.g. Google Drive, AWS S3, Cloudflare R2) just keep track data updates git using DVC. R package uses data files running code examples tests, rendering vignettes. simple dvc pull pull data remote storage allow processes take place.

title: Installation
text: Using {remotes} directly GitHub:
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tidywf/nemo") # latest main commit remotes::install_github("tidywf/nemo@v0.0.3.9012") # released version

title: Installation
text: conda package available tidywf channel https://anaconda.org/tidywf/r-nemo.
code: conda create \   -n nemo_env \   -c tidywf -c conda-forge \   r-nemo==0.0.3.9012  conda activate nemo_env

title: Installation
text: use Pixi, can create new isolated environment deployed conda package: can create task run nemo.R CLI script: activate environment use nemo directly R environment:
code: pixi init -c tidywf -c conda-forge ./tidy_env cd ./tidy_env pixi add r-nemo==0.0.3.9012 pixi task add nemo "nemo.R" pixi run nemo --help pixi shell R library(nemo)

title: Schema
text: pattern value must use YAML double-quoted strings backslash escapes processed correctly (e.g. "\\.tool1\\.table1\\.tsv$").
code: tables:   <table_name>:     description: '<human-readable description>'     pattern: "<regex matching the file basename>"     ftype: '<file type — see below>'     columns:       - raw: '<raw column name or positional label>'         tidy: '<tidy snake_case name>'         type: 'char | int | float'         description: '<human-readable description>'         versions: ['<version1>', '<version2>']

title: Schema
text: ftype field signals parsing strategy table. Tab-delimited default; delimiters handled inside specific parser, ftype. Standard delimited file header row. raw: names must exactly match column names file — used schema_guess identify versioned schema applies given file. header row; columns identified purely position. raw: names always X1, X2, …, XN (N = number columns). makes version tracking unambiguous: adding removing column reflected updating versions: array affected positional label. tidy step renames X1 => tidy_name_1, X2 => tidy_name_2, etc. positionally. special case txt-nohead exactly two columns first column key second value. file pivoted wide key becomes column name. raw: names actual key values (e.g. SampleID, TotalReads), matched schema_guess pivot. header; long format one column holds metric names adjacent columns hold values (e.g. count percentage). columns: block maps raw metric names tidy column names. structural logic (column key, _pct suffix generation, section cleaning) lives tool’s tidy_* method, schema.

text: Standard delimited file header row. raw: names must exactly match column names file — used schema_guess identify versioned schema applies given file.

title: Schema
text: header row; columns identified purely position. raw: names always X1, X2, …, XN (N = number columns). makes version tracking unambiguous: adding removing column reflected updating versions: array affected positional label. tidy step renames X1 => tidy_name_1, X2 => tidy_name_2, etc. positionally.

title: Schema
text: special case txt-nohead exactly two columns first column key second value. file pivoted wide key becomes column name. raw: names actual key values (e.g. SampleID, TotalReads), matched schema_guess pivot.

title: Schema
text: header; long format one column holds metric names adjacent columns hold values (e.g. count percentage). columns: block maps raw metric names tidy column names. structural logic (column key, _pct suffix generation, section cleaning) lives tool’s tidy_* method, schema.

title: Schema
text: versions: field column explicit array every tool version column appears . gives full picture column history, including additions removals across versions. effective schema version V columns whose versions array contains V. schema_guess uses automatically match file’s column names correct versioned snapshot. Tables single version (columns versions: [latest]) common case require special handling.
code: table1 columns:   SampleID   versions: [v1.2.3, v4.5.6, latest]  # present in all versions   Chromosome versions: [v1.2.3, v4.5.6, latest]   Start      versions: [v1.2.3, v4.5.6, latest]   End        versions: [v1.2.3, v4.5.6, latest]   metricX    versions: [v1.2.3]                   # dropped in v4.5.6   metricY    versions: [latest]                   # added in latest   metricZ    versions: [latest]

title: Structure
text: Config object reads single schema.yaml file inst/config/tools/<tool>/. table entry defines file pattern, file type, description, list columns — column carrying raw name (produced tool) tidy name (snake_case), type, description, versions array listing every tool version column appears . Config derives versioned raw tidy views unified source demand. See ?Config. intro patterns descriptions versions schemas Patterns used fish relevant files directory listing. Tool1 file patterns. Descriptions usually derived individual tool documentation. Tool1 file descriptions. versions array column lists every tool version column appears , giving full picture additions removals across versions. Tool1 schema versions. get_schemas_all() derives versioned view unified column list. Pass "raw" (default) "tidy" get column names either form:

text: Tool main organisation class file parsers tidiers. contains functions parsing tidying typical CSV/TSV files (column names), TXT files column names missing. Currently utilises simple readr::read_delim function {readr} package reads data memory. See ?Tool. simple parsers used 80-90% cases, future can optimise parsing needed faster packages {data.table}, {duckdb-r}/{duckplyr} {r-polars}. initialise config list tidy filter write nemofy can different Tool children classes inherit (override) functions fields Tool parent class. example, can create Tool object Tool1 follows: Initialise Tool1 object: Config object also constructed based name supplied - used internally find files interest infer schemas: can list files can parsed list_files(): can parse tidy files interest using tidy function. title: Tidy Bioinformatic Pipeline Outputs
text: {nemo} R package contains building blocks parsing, tidying, writing bioinformatic pipeline results consistent structure. short, traverses directory containing results one runs certain bioinformatic tools, parses files recognises, tidies (includes data reshaping, normalisation, column name cleanup etc.), writes output format choice e.g. Apache Parquet, PostgreSQL, TSV, RDS. specific tools can handle controlled configuration files written YAML part ‘child’ {nemo} packages, like {tidywigits} {dracarys}. configuration files (inst/config respective packages) specify schemas, types, patterns field descriptions raw input files tidy output tbls.

title: Tool Object — Tool
text: Base R6 class nemo tools. Subclasses implement parsers specific bioinformatic tool outputs optionally overriding parse_{tname}() tidy_{tname}() methods custom parse tidy logic per table. Tool object: name (name); path directory outputs (path); schema configuration Config object (config); tibble files matching Config patterns (files); tibble parsed tidied files (tbls); typical workflow : optionally filter files filter_files(), parse tidy tidy(), write outputs write(). nemofy() chains three steps.

title: Workflow Object — Workflow
text: Orchestrates multiple Tool objects shared directory, handling file discovery, filtering, tidying, writing single pipeline. Workflow object: name (name); one paths workflow results (path); list Tool subclass objects (tools); tibble files shared directory (files_tbl); tibble written output files, populated write() (written_files); typical workflow : optionally filter filter_files(), tidy tidy(), write write(). nemofy() chains three steps.

code: path <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo") x <- Tool$new("tool1", pkg = "nemo", path) schemas_all <- x$raw_schemas_all # table1: three versions with different column sets (d1_v123 <- parse_file(file.path(path, "v1.2.3", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv"), "table1", schemas_all)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>   SampleID Chromosome Start   End metricX #>   <chr>    <chr>      <int> <int>   <dbl> #> 1 sampleA  chr1          10    50     0.1 #> 2 sampleA  chr2         100   500     0.2 #> 3 sampleA  chr3        1000  5000     0.3 (d1_v456 <- parse_file(file.path(path, "v4.5.6", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv"), "table1", schemas_all)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   SampleID Chromosome Start   End #>   <chr>    <chr>      <int> <int> #> 1 sampleA  chr1          10    50 #> 2 sampleA  chr2         100   500 #> 3 sampleA  chr3        1000  5000 (d1_lat  <- parse_file(file.path(path, "latest", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv"), "table1", schemas_all)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   SampleID Chromosome Start   End metricY metricZ #>   <chr>    <chr>      <int> <int>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 sampleA  chr1          10    50     0.4     0.7 #> 2 sampleA  chr2         100   500     0.5     0.8 #> 3 sampleA  chr3        1000  5000     0.6     0.9 # table2: two versions (v1.0.0 drops metricB) (d2_v1  <- parse_file(file.path(path, "v1.0.0", "sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv"), "table2", schemas_all)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   SampleID metricA #>   <chr>    <chr>   #> 1 sampleA  a       #> 2 sampleA  b       #> 3 sampleA  c       (d2_lat <- parse_file(file.path(path, "latest", "sampleA.tool1.table2.tsv"), "table2", schemas_all)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   SampleID metricA metricB #>   <chr>    <chr>     <dbl> #> 1 sampleA  a         12.3  #> 2 sampleA  b          4.56 #> 3 sampleA  c          7.89

code: path <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo") x <- Tool$new("tool1", pkg = "nemo", path) schemas_all <- x$raw_schemas_all pname <- "table4" fpath_latest <- file.path(path, "latest", "sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv") fpath_v1 <- file.path(path, "v1.0.0", "sampleA.tool1.table4.tsv") schema_latest <- schemas_all |>   dplyr::filter(.data$name == pname, .data$version == "latest") |>   dplyr::select("version", "schema") schema_v1 <- schemas_all |>   dplyr::filter(.data$name == pname, .data$version == "v1.0.0") |>   dplyr::select("version", "schema") (d_latest <- parse_file_nohead(fpath_latest, schema_latest)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   X1      X2       X3    X4    X5 #>   <chr>   <chr> <int> <int> <dbl> #> 1 sampleA chr1    100   500  32.5 #> 2 sampleB chr2    200   800  18.1 (d_v1 <- parse_file_nohead(fpath_v1, schema_v1)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   X1      X2       X3 #>   <chr>   <chr> <int> #> 1 sampleA chr1    100 #> 2 sampleB chr2    200

title: Guess Schema — schema_guess
code: dir1 <- system.file("extdata/tool1", package = "nemo") fpath1 <- file.path(dir1, "latest", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv") fpath2 <- file.path(dir1, "v1.2.3", "sampleA.tool1.table1.tsv") pname <- "table1" cnames1 <- file_hdr(fpath1) cnames2 <- file_hdr(fpath2) conf <- Config$new("tool1", pkg = "nemo") schemas_all <- conf$get_schemas_all("raw") (s1 <- schema_guess(pname, cnames1, schemas_all)) #> $schema #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   field      type  #>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 SampleID   c     #> 2 Chromosome c     #> 3 Start      i     #> 4 End        i     #> 5 metricY    d     #> 6 metricZ    d     #>  #> $version #> [1] "latest" #>  (s2 <- schema_guess(pname, cnames2, schemas_all)) #> $schema #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   field      type  #>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 SampleID   c     #> 2 Chromosome c     #> 3 Start      i     #> 4 End        i     #> 5 metricX    d     #>  #> $version #> [1] "v1.2.3" #>
